headlines.title: U.S. stocks drift after oil prices ease and Big Tech stocks fall
Stock Market Today, June 22: Alphabet Slips on Key Departure and Defensive Blue Chips Gain
10-Year Treasury Yield Rises to 4.507% — Data Talk

notes: U.S. equities experienced a mixed close on Monday, June 22, 2026. A broad selloff in mega-cap technology stocks weighed heavily on the Nasdaq and S&P 500, while the Dow added gains due to strength in defensive blue chips.
Brent crude oil prices declined 3.2% amid diplomatic progress and peace talk optimism between the U.S. and Iran, potentially averting disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.
The 10-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.507% as markets monitored inflation metrics following recent oil price surges.
The Nasdaq-100 and S&P 500 quarterly rebalances took effect, ushering major AI and infrastructure players like CoreWeave and Astera Labs into the indices.
